title: Ru Murleen
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Rumurleen_imdaar.jpg]] |name=Ru Murleen |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her{{InsiderCite|27|LucasArts Update|Excerpts from the Journal of a Rebel Pilot}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]]''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]]{{CSWECite|II|343|Murleen, Ru}} |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|That was the first time I had a real conversation with her and found out that she wasn't only a great pilot and shooter, but that she was smart, funny, nice, beautiful… Well, forget that. I'm just lucky to know her and to be able to fly with her.|Rookie One|}} '''Ru Murleen''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who served as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Murleen was the youngest pilot to reach the rank of [[Commander/Legends|commander]] and became a pilot instructor at the Rebel [[Kolaador Base|training facility]] on [[Kolaador]], hoping to pass her skills on to others. Shortly after the [[Battle of Toprawa]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Murleen led a group of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] starfighters to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where [[Anchorhead Base]] was under [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|attack]] by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces, and later fought Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] during the evacuation of [[Gamma Base]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. She transferred to the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] soon after and fought against the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Murleen was later assigned to [[Imdaar Alpha]] to monitor Imperial activity on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. When the Alliance learned of an Imperial project to create [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaked]] starfighters, Murleen teamed up with her former student, [[Rookie One]], on a mission to infiltrate the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''[[Terror]]'' and steal one of the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantoms]]. The two Rebel agents not only acquired one of the new starfighters, but were able to use it to destroy the ''Terror'', along with the [[Imdaar Alpha research station|TIE Phantom factory]] at Imdaar Alpha, effectively putting an end to the project. ==Biography== ===Early career=== {{Quote|You impressed Commander Farrell. That's not easy. Me, I'm a little more forgiving, but the course ahead isn't. So follow me closely, and may the Force be with you.|audio=Murleen1-RA.flac|Ru Murleen to Rookie One|}} Ru Murleen joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Her skill and daring attitude helped her to move through the ranks quickly and,''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' manual by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' takes place at the same time as the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. she had been promoted to [[Commander/Legends|commander]]—the youngest in the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]] at the time. Hoping to pass on her skills to others, Murleen took a position as an instructor at a Rebel [[Kolaador Base|training facility]]''[[Strongholds of Resistance]]'' on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Kolaador]], a position she held for much of the base's existence. Murleen led rookie pilots in [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearheads]] through the planet's canyons, whose [[Crystal/Legends|crystalline]] spires formed a natural obstacle course. While serving on Kolaador, she trained many new pilots for the Alliance, who went on to serve throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault: The Official Insider's Guide]]'' ===On the run=== ====Battle of Tatooine==== {{Quote|Well done, Rookie One. We can use more pilots like you. Follow me to base, and we'll get you assigned to a squadron. And while we're waiting, I'll let you buy me a drink for that fancy flying.|Ru Murleen to Rookie One|audio=Murleen3-RA.flac|}} [[File:KolaadorBar.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Murleen shares a drink with Rookie One in the pilot's bar at Kolaador Rebel base.]] Among her students was "[[Rookie One]]," who undertook the final phase of his training under her instruction shortly after the [[Battle of Toprawa]], during which the Rebel Alliance stole [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] to the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], a [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] with enough power to destroy a planet. Murleen was impressed by the young pilot's skill and, after he passed his canyon run, she joined him for a celebratory drink at the Rebel base while he awaited assignment to a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]]. However, their drink was cut short by the news that [[Anchorhead Base]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Rookie One's base for his earlier training, was under [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|attack]] by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces searching for the stolen plans. Anchorhead Base was unable to hold off the Imperial forces for long, so Murleen assembled a [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] and headed to Tatooine to assist them. To join her on the mission, she selected Rookie One and [[Thurlow Harris]]. When the group arrived in the [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo system]], they found an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of Tatooine. Knowing that the group's chances on the surface would be slim with the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] coordinating the attack, Murleen led her group in an attack on its command center, hoping to eliminate the threat before proceeding down to Tatooine. The group [[Starfighter combat/Legends|engaged]] [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] launched by the Star Destroyer, while strafing the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] to weaken its defenses. When the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] protecting the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] finally fell, Murleen instructed Rookie One to arm his [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] and commence an attack run on the unprotected command tower. Rookie One scored a direct hit, crippling the vessel. With the threat eliminated, the group quickly proceeded to the surface to help the Rebels below. ====Relief at Anchorhead and Hoth==== [[File:Murleen.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Murleen shortly before the Battle of Yavin]] After entering the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], Murleen contacted [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Turland Hack]] at Anchorhead Base for a status report. Hack informed the group that the base was already under attack from three TIE fighters. Despite their best efforts, the group was unable to reach the base in time, and it was destroyed moments later. The attacking TIE fighters were now alerted to the presence of the Rebel reinforcements, and Murleen took her group into Beggar's Canyon to eliminate them before they could report to their commanders. After all three were eliminated, the Rebels were joined by the X-wing of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Merrick Simms]]. Imperial ground forces were already moving into [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], and Murleen ordered Harris and Rookie One to join Simms in engaging the [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]] and Imperial [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|landing craft]]. Meanwhile, Murleen attempted to cut off the Imperial supply lines, and instructed the others to withdraw once the situation got too dangerous, and to rendezvous with her at the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|fleet]], before proceeding to [[Gamma Base]] on the ice-world of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' manual ([[Wikipedia:Sega CD|Sega CD]]) All four Rebel pilots made it safely to Gamma Base. However, their escape would be short-lived. The [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], determined to locate where they had fled to, had [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droids]] dispatched throughout the [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sector]]. When the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] discovered Gamma Base, Imperial forces moved to [[Battle of Hoth (0 BBY)|attack]] it and the Rebels were forced to evacuate. Murleen led an X-wing group charged with protecting the last Rebel [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] to leave while it attempted to escape to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Joined by Rookie One, who was delayed in leaving the planet, the group engaged waves of TIE fighters, keeping them away from the transport until it safely jumped out of the [[Hoth system/Legends|system]]. Once the Rebel pilots were clear of the Imperial fighters, they followed the transport to safety. ===Battle of Yavin=== Murleen and the other Rebels fled to the main Rebel [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on the Outer Rim [[Moon/Legends|moon]]''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. However, they were still not out of danger. Although the Alliance had been able to acquire the plans to the Death Star, the Empire had learned the location of their base and the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] was en route to Yavin. Fortunately for the Rebels, analysis of the plans indicated a weakness which would make the battlestation vulnerable to a starfighter attack.{{Film|IV}} Murleen was assigned to [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] for the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], along with Rookie One and Thurlow Harris, under the command of Merrick Simms. As the squadron approached the battlestation, a Star Destroyer in the Death Star's support fleet launched TIE fighters to intercept them, but Blue Squadron was able to eliminate the threat and proceed through the perimeter. After joining up with pilots from [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red]] and [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadrons]], Simms led Blue Squadron towards the Death Star itself, before instructing Murleen and Rookie One to accelerate to attack speed and commence strafing runs on the Death Star's [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries. The battle plan called for navigating a trench along the surface in order to reach a [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]]—a direct hit from a proton torpedo would trigger a chain reaction to destroy the station. However, before the assault on the trench could begin, Simms dispatched Murleen and Rookie One to eliminate a [[Death Star cannon|heavy cannon]] situated near the exhaust port. Since the cannon was too heavily armored to attack directly, the pilots opened a gap in its shielding and flew into the superstructure, where they destroyed several power relays, thus disabling the weapon, before regrouping with the others. With the threat of the heavy cannon neutralized, the way was clear for Alliance forces to begin their trench runs. Meanwhile, Blue Squadron was charged with attacking the Death Star's [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] directly, in an attempt to both damage it and divert attention away from Red and Gold Squadrons while they attacked the exhaust port.''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' The initial trench runs failed to hit the target and time was running out when [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] of Red Squadron succeeded in hitting the target, delivering the killing blow to the Death Star. ===Infiltrating the ''Terror''=== {{Quote|Aren't you a little short for a stormtrooper?''"
"''Shut up.|Rookie One and Ru Murleen shortly after leaving the ''Epsilon Four''|audio=RookieOneMurleen-RAII.flac|}} [[File:Imdaar.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Murleen awaiting Rookie One on Imdaar]] Murleen turned thirty around this time.{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_stry.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Original Story}} At some point following the Battle of Yavin, she was assigned to the planet [[Imdaar/Legends|Imdaar]] to report on any Imperial presence. She began to notice high levels of Imperial fleet activity near the planet and its [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Imdaar Alpha]], leading her to conclude that the Empire was up to something in the area but was unable to determine what they were planning. Shortly after the Battle of Hoth in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Rookie One helped the Alliance uncover evidence that the Empire was working to develop [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaked]] starfighters, which they could use to devastate the Alliance Fleet. When the Rebels learned that the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] ''[[Terror]]'' was en route to Imdaar, they theorized that it was preparing to deploy a full [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]] of the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|Phantom TIEs]]. Alliance [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] [[Mission to Imdaar|dispatched]] Rookie One to Imdaar, instructing him to rendezvous with their agent, infiltrate the ''Terror'', and retrieve a TIE Phantom. The Rebel Alliance hoped that, by studying one of the fighters, they could devise a way to counter the technology before it was used against them. Rookie One made the journey to Imdaar in a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]], making his way past Imperial defenses and rendezvousing with Murleen in a swamp on the planet. Not knowing who had been sent to join her, Murleen was surprised to see her friend emerge from the starfighter. The two had little time to catch up, however, as the ''Terror'' was due to arrive at any moment. In order to infiltrate the ''Terror'', they would first need to gain access to the planetary [[Imdaar landing platform|supply platform]], where Murleen hoped to steal a cargo shuttle to get aboard the Star Destroyer. The supply base's landing platform was on the other side of the swamp, but Murleen provided [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]] which allowed them to make good time. After arriving at the base, they incapacitated the guards and, after assuming the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]' armor, stole the ''Lambda''-class cargo shuttle ''[[Epsilon Four]]''. [[File:RookieOne6.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Rookie One and Ru Murleen stealing a TIE Phantom]] The Rebels took the stolen shuttle to the coordinates where they expected to find the ''Terror''. When they could see no sign of it, Murleen tried hailing the vessel and got a response. After transmitting the shuttle's security code, they were given permission to land and the ''Terror'', which was also fitted with the cloaking technology, appeared before them. The Rebels took the shuttle into the ''Terror''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] and, using their stormtrooper disguises, began searching for the TIE Phantoms. When they tried to enter a restricted area, their lack of security clearance brought them to the attention of an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (Terror)|Imperial officer]] who raised the alarm. With the Imperials alerted to their presence, the Rebels raced toward their objective but were soon stopped by a sealed door. Murleen was able to force a manual override to give them access and the pair continued to fight their way past stormtroopers until they reached a chasm and were unable to activate the bridge. Eventually coming to a dead end, and with the Imperials in close pursuit, they were forced to seek an alternate route through the sewer on the deck below. Although they had temporarily escaped their pursuers, Murleen knew the Imperials would not take long to find them. The pair moved quickly through the dark sewer tunnels but were soon intercepted by an Imperial search party. Fighting their way through the stormtroopers, Murleen covered the rear while Rookie One fought those to the front until they eventually came to a freight elevator to the service hangar. After riding the elevator up, the Rebels evaded the hangar's guards and boarded one of the TIE Phantoms. Murleen took the pilot's seat in the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]], leaving Rookie One to act as gunner. Murleen managed to get the fighter underway and they began to navigate through the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer's]] massive hangars. However, the Imperials quickly sealed the hangar doors to prevent their escape, forcing Murleen to fly the stolen fighter through the superstructure in search of another way out—a search which eventually led them to the ship's [[Reactor|main reactor]]. Realizing that they had the opportunity to destroy the Terror and its complement of TIE Phantoms, Murleen instructed Rookie One to target the reactor's [[beam focuser]]s. When the reactor went critical, Murleen flew the fighter out of the ''Terror'' as explosions began to erupt throughout it. ===End of the TIE Phantom=== {{Quote|That's where they're making the phantom TIEs! There must be thousands of them in there. If we don't do something about them, it doesn't matter if we get this thing home or not!''"
"''Uh… what do you have in mind?''"
"''Maybe it's better if you don't know. Just hang on and don't let go of those blaster controls.|Ru Murleen and Rookie One|audio=RookieOneMurleen2-RAII.flac|}} [[File:TIEPhantom2.png|thumb|left|250px|Murleen pilots the TIE Phantom.]] Once clear of the ''Terror'', Murleen began to plot a course to the rendezvous point. However, they were intercepted by TIE Phantoms, which cut off their escape route, forcing Murleen to bring the fighter around and head back towards the ''Terror'', skimming along the Star Destroyer's hull while attempting to evade fire from the incoming [[TIE series/Legends|TIEs]]. Rookie One fought off waves of TIE Phantoms, but the situation was looking bleak before Murleen managed to activate the cloaking device, hiding the fighter from the Imperials. Before the Rebels could leave the [[Star system/Legends|system]], however, the ''Terror'' was destroyed in an explosion which caused the previously cloaked [[Imdaar Alpha research station|factory]] on Imdaar Alpha, to which the Star Destroyer was docked, to become visible. Murleen realized that the facility was the source of the TIE Phantoms—with the factory capable of producing thousands of the fighters, the stolen one would do little to aid the Rebels in combating the threat. Knowing that they needed to stop the TIE Phantoms quickly, Murleen took the stolen fighter into the factory, negotiating the hazardous tunnels inside until the Rebels arrived at the main reactor. Rookie One fired at the reactor, causing it to destabilize, before Murleen took them to safety, moments before the facility was destroyed. With the threat of the TIE Phantoms effectively eliminated, the Rebels returned to base with the stolen fighter, holding high hopes that the technology could be adapted to provide Alliance fighters with an advantage over the Empire. However, the fighter [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destructed]] shortly after arrival, but despite the loss of the fighter, the mission was a major success and the Rebels were given a warm reception, with Admiral [[Krane (Rebel Alliance)|Krane]] leading the commendations. Following their return, Murleen and Rookie One became romantically involved. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Rookie One, is that you?''"
"''In the flesh. Can you use another hand?''"
"''Well, no…but I can sure use another pilot.|Ru Murleen and Rookie One|audio=MurleenRookie-RA.flac|}} Ru Murleen was a skilled and daring starfighter pilot who was quick, bright, and had a sarcastic sense of humor. Eager to pass on her skills to others, she helped train many new pilots and displayed an eye for talent. This, combined with her quick wit, kept her rookies alert and helped her gain the respect and friendship of those she worked with. Recognizing that piloting was her primary skill, Murleen originally avoided participating in activities that kept her out of the cockpit, preferring to serve the Alliance exclusively as a pilot, and her life was centered around flying. Later in her career, however, she began to take on other assignments. Murleen first met Rookie One early in his Rebel career, when she acted as one of his instructors during his training. She was impressed with his skill as a pilot and the two became friends, sharing a drink after his assessment. Rookie One started to feel attracted to her during this time, but it was not until over three years later, after the two worked together to defeat the TIE Phantom project, that they acted on their mutual attraction. Murleen was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] with light skin, [[Color/Legends|blue]] eyes, and brown hair. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|I'm hit! It came from behind! I can't stay with you.''"
"''Pull out, Blue Two.''"
"''Sorry. May the Force be with you.|Ru Murleen and Merrick Simms, as Murleen withdraws from the trench run during the Battle of Yavin|audio=MurleenSimms-RA.flac|}} [[File:Collette_Michaud-Adventurer_9.jpg|150px|thumb|Collette Michaud, the basis for Murleen's original appearance]] Ru Murleen was created by [[Vincent Lee]] for the [[1993]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]''. Her in-game appearance was based on [[Collette Michaud]], the director of the Art Department at LucasArts, while she was named after [[Ru Murleen (real person)|Ru Murleen]], the actress who provided her voice for the game. The character's backstory was expanded in ''Rebel Assault''{{'s}} accompanying manual. Murleen later returned in Lee's ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'', the [[1995]] sequel to ''Rebel Assault'', in which she was portrayed by actress [[Julie Eccles]]. If Rookie One is killed during the levels "Aboard the Terror" and "The Sewers" in the latter game, a "game over" cutscene shows her mourning Rookie One saying "Kid!". [[File:RookieOneBTS.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Jamison Jones]] and Julie Eccles on the set of ''Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire'']] Lee's storyboards and scripts document for ''Rebel Assault II'', dated [[November 16]], [[1994]], included a brief description of Murleen.''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' Although not included in the game or its manual, the information appeared on the LucasArts website and the document was later printed in [[Rob Smith]]'s reference book ''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]''. Lee also made notes on the people he thought symbolized each character in the game, once again likening Murleen to Collette Michaud. Murleen was briefly mentioned in "[[LucasArts Update|''Excerpts from the Journal of a Rebel Pilot'']]," a promotional article written by [[Sue Seserman]] for ''Rebel Assault II'', published in 1995 in the [[Star Wars Insider 27|twenty-seventh issue]] of the magazine ''[[Star Wars Insider]]''.{{InsiderCite|27|LucasArts Update|Excerpts from the Journal of a Rebel Pilot}} She was also mentioned in the [[2005]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', which discusses the events depicted in ''Rebel Assault II'', but refers to Murleen and Rookie One only as "Rebel saboteurs." Murleen received an entry in the [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ===Battle of Yavin=== ''Rebel Assault'' depicts Murleen, as part of Blue Squadron, taking part in a trench run at the Battle of Yavin, contradicting the battle as depicted in the [[1977]] [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. The trench run in the game is almost identical to that of the movie, with only the persons involved being different. The game depicts Blue Squadron attempting a trench run with Simms entering first while Murleen joins Rookie One and Harris in providing cover. As they negotiate down the long trench, the group is pursued by three TIE fighters, including a [[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|TIE Advanced x1]] which was presumably intended to be the one used by Vader in the film. The TIEs open fire, killing Harris and forcing Murleen to pull out, echoing the fates of [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]] and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] in the film. Finally, it is Rookie One's shot that is depicted as destroying the battlestation, as opposed to Skywalker's. In [[December]] [[2006]], [[Leland Chee]] commented on the [[StarWars.com]] [[StarWars.com Message Boards|Message Boards]] that Rookie One's trench run was non-canonical.{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1062#16021160|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-12-07|time=6:15 PM|quote=Rookiee One's tench run in non-continuity.}} In [[2012]], the reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' confirmed that Blue Squadron was assigned to attack the Death Star's superlaser while Red and Gold Squadrons attacked the trench. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' ==Sources== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Ru Murleen|imagecat=Images of Ru Murleen}} *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault: The Official Insider's Guide]]'' *{{GalaxyCite|4|Around the Galaxy}} *{{AdventurerCite|10|Rebel Assault II: Lights, Camera, Action|''Rebel Assault II'': Lights, Camera, Action}} *{{GalaxyCite|5|Game Room|Game Room: Rebel Rouser}} *{{InsiderCite|27|LucasArts Update|Excerpts from the Journal of a Rebel Pilot}} *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II Official Player's Guide]]'' *{{JournalCite|9|New Horizons}} *{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_stry.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Original Story}} *{{GalaxyCite|13|Communiqués}} *{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} {{Imo}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{FactFile2013|1|YAV 1-4|The Battle of Yavin}} *{{FactFile2014|1|0 ABY 45-48|The Battle of Yavin}} *''[[Strongholds of Resistance]]'' *{{InsiderCite|191|Jedi Master's Quiz}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Ru Murleen |es=Ru Murleen }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Murleen, Ru}} [[Category:Blue Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]]
